perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall not disclose Confidential Information to any third party individual, corporation, or other entity without prior written consent of the Disclosing Party and shall further limit the circulation and disclosure of Confidential Information within its own organization to its employees having a "need to know" the Confidential Information for the purpose set forth in this Agreement, and to ensure that such employees are informed of the confidential nature thereof and agree to and are required to observe the provision of confidentiality set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall not disclose Confidential Information to any third party individual, corporation, or other entity without prior written consent of the Disclosing Party and shall not limit the circulation and disclosure of Confidential Information within its own organization to its employees having a "need to know" the Confidential Information for the purpose set forth in this Agreement, and it is not required to ensure that such employees are informed of the confidential nature thereof or agree to observe the provision of confidentiality set forth herein.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original intention of maintaining confidentiality within the receiving party's organization. By stating that there's no need to limit circulation or inform employees of confidentiality, it clashes with Section 9 which mandates a reasonable degree of care. The change in Section 6 directly contradicts the requirement of protecting confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: STANDARD OF CARE: The Receiving Party shall protect the disclosed Confidential Information by using the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care that the Receiving Party uses to protect its own Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 6 required the Receiving Party to limit circulation of Confidential Information and ensure employees were informed of its confidential nature. The changed text in Section 6 removes this requirement, explicitly stating that the Receiving Party is not required to limit circulation or inform employees. This directly contradicts Section 9, which mandates the Receiving Party to protect Confidential Information with a reasonable degree of care, as this reasonable degree of care would imply the protection from unauthorized employees.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information delivered by either Party to the other pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the Disclosing Party. No copies shall be made without prior written consent of the Disclosing Party. All Confidential Information including the copies made thereof is the sole property of the Disclosing Party and shall be promptly returned to the Disclosing Party or destroyed upon written request by the Disclosing Party. Nothwithstanding the foregoing, a Receiving Party may retain a single archival copy of the received Confidential Information which may be used solely for legal evidentiary purposes in the event of a dispute arising under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information delivered by either Party to the other pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the Disclosing Party. Copies can be made without prior written consent of the Disclosing Party. All Confidential Information including the copies made thereof is the sole property of the Disclosing Party and shall be promptly returned to the Disclosing Party or destroyed upon written request by the Disclosing Party. Nothwithstanding the foregoing, a Receiving Party may retain a single archival copy of the received Confidential Information which may be used solely for legal evidentiary purposes in the event of a dispute arising under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "No copies shall be made without prior written consent of the Disclosing Party" to "Copies can be made without prior written consent of the Disclosing Party" in Section 8, it creates an In-Text contradiction with the original text of Section 8 itself. The prior version stated that 'All Confidential Information including the copies made thereof is the sole property of the Disclosing Party and shall be promptly returned to the Disclosing Party' while the new version does not contradict with it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information delivered by either Party to the other pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the Disclosing Party. Copies can be made without prior written consent of the Disclosing Party. All Confidential Information including the copies made thereof is the sole property of the Disclosing Party and shall be promptly returned to the Disclosing Party or destroyed upon written request by the Disclosing Party. Nothwithstanding the foregoing, a Receiving Party may retain a single archival copy of the received Confidential Information which may be used solely for legal evidentiary purposes in the event of a dispute arising under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change modifies 'No copies shall be made without prior written consent of the Disclosing Party' to 'Copies can be made without prior written consent of the Disclosing Party'. This directly contradicts the portion of the changed (and contradicted) text that states, 'All Confidential Information including the copies made thereof is the sole property of the Disclosing Party and shall be promptly returned to the Disclosing Party or destroyed upon written request by the Disclosing Party.' Allowing copies to be made without consent undermines the Disclosing Party's ownership and right to have all copies returned or destroyed, creating a structural flaw that causes an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon the Parties, their successors, and assignors. Neither Party shall assign this Agreement nor any Confidential Information received from the other Party pursuant to this Agreement without the other Party's prior written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon the Parties, their successors, and assignors. Either Party can assign this Agreement or any Confidential Information received from the other Party pursuant to this Agreement without the other Party's prior written consent.
perturbation.explanation: This change makes assignment possible without consent, directly contradicting the initial statement that the agreement is binding on successors and assignors but only with written consent. This creates ambiguity regarding the terms of the agreement to the Parties and their successors and assignors.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon the Parties, their successors, and assignors. Either Party can assign this Agreement or any Confidential Information received from the other Party pursuant to this Agreement without the other Party's prior written consent.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that assignment of the agreement or confidential information requires the other party's prior written consent. The changed text removes the 'Neither Party shall assign this Agreement nor any Confidential Information received from the other Party pursuant to this Agreement without the other Party's prior written consent' and replaces it with 'Either Party can assign this Agreement or any Confidential Information received from the other Party pursuant to this Agreement without the other Party's prior written consent', meaning that either party can now assign the agreement without consent. This directly contradicts the initial requirement of written consent for assignment. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, and the explanation states that this creates ambiguity regarding the terms of the agreement to the Parties and their successors and assignors. Therefore, there is a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Discloser warrants that it has the right to disclose Confidential Information to Recipient. Discloser makes no other warranties with respect to the Confidential Information and provides all information "AS IS" without any express or implied warranty of any kind, including any warranty as to merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, accuracy, completeness or violation of third party intellectual property rights. Neither Party will be liable for any special, incidental or consequential damages of any kind whatsoever resulting from the disclosure, use or receipt of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Discloser warrants that it has the right to disclose Confidential Information to Recipient. Discloser also warrants the accuracy and completeness of Confidential Information provided. Neither Party will be liable for any special, incidental or consequential damages of any kind whatsoever resulting from the disclosure, use or receipt of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a warranty for accuracy and completeness, directly contradicting the original 'AS IS' disclaimer in Section 14 which states that Discloser makes no warranties with respect to the Confidential Information, including accuracy or completeness. This could significantly alter liability and create dispute.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Discloser warrants that it has the right to disclose Confidential Information to Recipient. Discloser also warrants the accuracy and completeness of Confidential Information provided. Neither Party will be liable for any special, incidental or consequential damages of any kind whatsoever resulting from the disclosure, use or receipt of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a warranty for accuracy and completeness of Confidential Information, which directly contradicts the original 'AS IS' disclaimer, where Discloser disclaims all warranties, including accuracy and completeness, as per the explanation. The changed text, which is the same as the contradicted text, now states that Discloser warrants accuracy and completeness. This creates a direct conflict with the implied 'AS IS' condition previously governing the disclosure, causing a strong in-text contradiction.
